==== Auto-filled cached password. Welcome, {{USERNAME}}. ==== {{Class 3}} Level 735 is the 736th level of the Backrooms{{Blurquote|image=sunbanner.jpg|quote-text="A person's eyes are their primary means of understanding reality, so without them, perception falls apart. To lose the eyes is the cruelest fate."|person=}} === Description === {{Blurimage|file=BlindingMists.jpg|caption=An image taken from the level. Cameras have difficulty piercing the mists.}} Level 735, known colloquially as the “Blinding Mists”, takes the form of a neighborhood typical to what would be seen in suburban America. Two suns overlook the level, casting a colorless illumination over its landscape. Accompanying this is the secondary oddity of a persistent and impenetrable fog settled into the level, averaging 32 meters in depth. When one enters Level 735, they will find themselves standing in the middle of an empty and sun-bleached street littered with potholes. Both sides of this street are bordered by sidewalks, with streetlamps spaced every 100 meters. These streetlamp emit a washed-out orange glow, and, like the light from the suns, highlight the fog's presence rather than do anything to pierce through its obscurity. These sidewalks serve as a buffer between the road and well-manicured lawns, upon which sit homes of identical make to one another-- they are all painted an inoffensive beige, paired with dark grey roofing and white trim around the frames of windows and doors. Each of these homes has a singular floor, the interiors all possessing the same musty carpet and peeling wallpaper. Said wallpaper bears floral patterns, depicting species of plant life that have not been identified as any known in the Frontrooms. However, they will feel familiar, in a manner akin to a memory stored just out of reach. Every aspect of the interior is covered in a thin layer of dust. Furniture within these homes is sparse, often limited to a few couches or chairs that are almost always placed in haphazard manners that elicit a feeling of wrongness. Sofas are often seen pulled out from the walls at odd angles and tables have been seen set upside down with yet fully set with empty plates, a hollow mimicry of normalcy orchestrated by the level. Despite its best attempts, this place will not feel right, and one will immediately become wary. However, these indoor spaces are the most safe, and thus this feeling of paranoia should be ignored to the best of one's abilities. The homes themselves are of poor, structurally unsound construction. Most possess attics, though these should not be entered, as they are the most unstable and often cave in, and holes in the roofing often allow the mist from outside to fill these spaces. However, the houses of Level 735 possess basements, which are far more secure; while they are not entirely devoid of instability and risk, they often bear storage shelving which has been known to hold [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and canned foodstuffs. Given such resources being present, and the lower likelihood of collapse, basements are often worth entering, so long as proper caution is exercised and proper protocols are followed. Level 735 lacks a diurnal cycle, instead remaining in a state of permanent daytime. The level’s dual suns emit both an immense heat and a constant thrumming. They appear to be unstable to a notable degree, prone to solar winds and violent mass ejections. During the worst of these anomalies, Level 735’s temperatures spike with the highest recorded temperature lying at 58°C. It is presumed that the fog acts as a humid insulator, and during the worst solar events, outdoor portions of the level become uninhabitable. During these events, the hum sounding from Level 735's suns has been known to rise in volume immensely, with the loudest spike measuring in at 170 decibels. This volume is significant enough to cause damage to the ears including ruptured eardrums and general hearing loss. It is advised, due to a number of factors, to remain within the home structures of this level for as much time as is possible whilst one is within Level 735, and that an exit be utilized as soon as one is located. These structures seem to block out large amounts of heat and sound, and keep the fog at bay. Accompanying the risk of heat exhaustion, heat stroke, and damage to auditory perception, is the far more glaring danger of the following: === Chemical Mist === It is imperative to mention an anomaly resulting from a combination of aspects within Level 735 including extreme temperatures, intense light, and an unknown chemical compound present within the fogs. This compound is highly alkaline, yet contains an atom not yet fully documented, thus an exact chemical formula will not yet be detailed here. It is a corrosive irritant, being extremely harmful to human flesh. When the level's temperatures remain at reasonable levels, this substance is merely that-- an irritant. However, during the worst of solar events, when the fog holds onto excess amounts of heat energy, the chemical vapors become more reactive, and thus more prone to cause injury. During the highest recorded temperature spikes, there have been reports of wanderers caught outside being chemically "cooked alive" within the fog, though these cases are rare. More common, however, is the issue of ocular injury, which occurs even at lower temperatures. The chemicals within Level 735's mists tend to cling to the moisture in the eyes, binding to the water in the eyes' tear film. With long-term exposure, this can become catastrophic. Graphics of the different levels of damage along with accompanying descriptions has been included for organizational purposes. |-| General Irritation= {{Blurimage|file=Eyedamage1.png|caption=Artist's representation of '''General Irritation'''.}} Short-term exposure to the level's conditions can cause mild irritation to the skin and facial orifices, though the eyes often take the brunt of this damage. This is the stage in which the chemical mist binds to the fluid tear film on the ocular surface. At this stage, symptoms can be rather mild, as is the case with most alkaline chemical injuries, but the actual severity of discomfort varies between individuals. At this stage, one can expect to experience eye pain, excessive tearing, redness and inflammation, light sensitivity, a stinging or burning sensation, and blurred vision. This level of injury can occur at the level's base temperatures, even in the absence of solar events to raise the heat. At this stage, damage can still be halted with a thorough cleansing. It is advised, once these symptoms are experienced, to get within a house structure as soon as possible and to rinse one's eyes thoroughly with any water or Almond Water on hand. This mild damage will naturally heal itself in the absence of irritants. |-| Corrosion and Necrosis= {{Blurimage|file=Eyedamage2.png|caption=Artist's representation of '''Corrosion & Necrosis'''.}} As the mist within Level 735 is extremely alkaline, one will begin to suffer from alkaline chemical burns upon further exposure to the fog, especially when it is heated. Alkaline compounds are more dangerous to human tissues than acidic compounds, as such bases will quickly dissolve fats and proteins on the ocular surface in a process known as saponification. From this point, the chemical will penetrate structures buried deeper in the eyeball, causing liquefaction necrosis and extensive corrosion of tissues. One may not experience extreme pain even at this stage, due to the rapid penetration of alkaline chemicals leaving hydroxyl ions to denature collagen proteins in the cornea, leading to destruction that doesn't always come accompanied by a matching level of pain, If left untreated, the injured individual will be left with vision issues such as the cornea scarring and becoming totally or partially opaque, chronic dry eye, damage to deeper eye structures such as the lens and ciliary body, fluid buildup within the eye leading to intraocular pressure that could potentially cause glaucoma, and permanent vision loss. At this stage, it is imperative to fully rinse the eye for fifteen to twenty minutes, then seek medical attention as swiftly as is possible. |-| Ocular Melting= {{Blurimage|file=Eyedamage3.png|caption=Artist's representation of '''Ocular Melting'''.}} In extreme cases of long-term chemical exposure paired with the Level 735's extreme heat, a phenomenon known as "ocular melting" has occurred. At this point, large quantities of the eye's fattier tissues have saponified, softening the organ. From this point, the ocular structures will lose their shapes and stray from their proper alignments-- the lens will skew, the pupil will lose its proper shape, etcetera. The sclera will begin to liquify, and in extreme cases, the eye will then become unbound from its socket and drip down the face in a slurry. Upon resolidification, the injured individual will most likely find themselves afflicted with a condition known as symblepharon, which refers to a specific burn-induced injury in which one's eye has become fused to the eyelids. This will limit eye movement and the ability to close one's eyes, and requires a surgical remedy. Vision is entirely unsalvageable once a person experiences ocular melting, thus treatment does not involve attempting to keep the eye intact. One should seek medical attention and have the eyes removed through surgery. Post-operation, the primary objective of the injured individual should be to maintain a sterile healing environment for the remaining tissues to avoid complications such as infection and sepsis. === Recovered Log of Travel === The following is a Wanderer's account of their time within Level 735, transcribed from a journal found in an attic within the level. Day 178: I clipped into a new level today, on washed-out pavement and couldn't see a thing. This fog is impenetrable, and it stings like all hell. It's making my eyes water. It hurts my lungs just to breathe. I'm pretty sure there's something in the air. Day 179: I worked up the courage to enter one of the houses on the side of that main road, and there was one of those [[Entity 8|dog-man creatures]] in the basement. I locked it down there and went back upstairs. I was going to leave, but it got so hot outside-- a horrible humid heat-- that I suppose I have to risk staying here. There was definitely something in the fog out there, because the skin on my face is blistering and peeling. My eyes won't stop watering, like I'm constantly crying. When I put my hands to my face, they feel hot to the touch. Day 180: At least, I think it's day 180. The sun doesn't set here, like in most of these [[Level List|hellscapes]], so I can't know for sure. Anyway, the heat outside dulled a bit, so I worked up the courage to go back out to try to find an exit. I have a day of travel ahead of me. I caught my reflection in a fogged-up window pane and my eyes are red around the rims and inflamed, angry-looking. And either I can't see too well due to the fog, or my vision is worsening. They feel hot in their sockets, like miniature suns. I still can't stop crying. Day 181: It hurts. I can't see. I shouldn't have come back out here. [Undecipherable] see anything and everything hurts. [Undecipherable] mistake and my eyes [undecipherable] like gelatin and they're burning [undecipherable] The author's penmanship became very difficult to read during the final entry, lines crossing over eachother and letters becoming misshapen likely as a result of vision loss. The author's status is unknown, though they are presumed dead due to the lack of subsequent journal entries and high probability of complications such as infection. === Entities === * [[Entity 8|Hounds]] have been known to reside within basements of homes on Level 735. * [[Entity 9|Facelings]] have been seen driving cars down the main road. They do not stop for passerby, nor pause after a collision. It is advised not to travel on the road due to this. * [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] have been known to take up residence within attics or congregate on streetlamps. * Survivors have reported to have seen [https://backrooms-wiki.wikidot.com/entity-22 Warning Kites] flying before particularly lethal spikes in temperatures. === Outposts === Due to the unique climate of Level 735, casualties related to it are high and large colonies are rather difficult to maintain. However, there have been known to be small coalitions of individuals residing within larger buildings and aiding each-other with survival in the short term. Most of these groups have friendly to outsiders, often being willing to trade information regarding level exits for valuable resources. Among the ranks of some of these groups, there occasionally are medical professionals or individuals with rudimentary medical knowledge sufficient to treat wounds caused by the level's chemical mists. It is cautiously advised to seek aid from these groups if one finds themself cut off from any other means of getting medical attention. === Entrances === * Wandering down fog-heavy streets within [[Level 9]] has been known to lead here. * Entering out-of-place suburban homes in [[Level 11]] can cause one to noclip here. * === Exits === * Interacting with streetlamps has been known to lead one back to [[Level 11]] * Noclipping through a damp spot in a carpeted floor can lead one to [[Level 0]] * Wandering excessive distances into the fog has been known to lead one to [[Level 10]] * Remaining in basements for excessive amounts of time has been known to lead one to [[Level 8]] {{Accordion | animation-duration = 250 | accordion-1-title = Page Credits | accordion-1-contents = Written by [[User:LuminousFiction|LuminousFiction]].
All images taken and created by the author.
Much of the medical knowledge sourced from [https://www.insightvisionoc.com/primary-eye-care/eye-emergencies/alkaline-eye-injuries-immediate-steps-and-comprehensive-understanding/ Insight Vision Center's website].}} {{Redirect|Prev=734|Next=736}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]]
